# S-Expression Language: Form Reference

<!-- Auto-generated by src/docgen.zig from the evaluator's own
     dispatch tables. Do not edit by hand вҖ” run `zig build docs`
     to regenerate; `zig build test` fails when this file is stale. -->

Every special form, builtin operator, fmt directive, numeric-literal
suffix, and design-scope form the toolchain recognises, with arity
contracts and one-line summaries. The hand-written prose lives in
[`sexp-language.md`](sexp-language.md); this file is the
machine-checked grammar surface.

## Special forms

Arguments are passed un-evaluated; each form decides what to evaluate.

| Form | Arity | Summary |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `(let name expr)` | 2 | Bind `name` to the evaluated value of `expr` in the current scope. |
| `(if cond then else)` | 3 | Short-circuit conditional. Only the matching branch is evaluated. |
| `(import nameвҖҰ)` | 1+ | Load library components or modules by name. Searches `lib/components/` then `lib/modules/`. |
| `(defmodule name (param \| (param default)вҖҰ) ["docstring"] bodyвҖҰ)` | 2+ | Define a parameterised module that closes over the surrounding env. A `(param default)` pair makes the argument optional вҖ” its default evaluates at call time when omitted, so a fully-defaulted module also renders standalone. |
| `(design-block "name" formвҖҰ)` | 1+ | The root container вҖ” every `.sexp` design file evaluates to one. |
| `(block "name" formвҖҰ \| name (param \| (param default)вҖҰ) ["docstring"] bodyвҖҰ)` | 1+ | The unified circuit definition. A string name is an eager design root (identical to `(design-block вҖҰ)`); a bare-atom name with a parameter list is a parameterised, embeddable definition (identical to `(defmodule вҖҰ)`). `(design-block вҖҰ)` and `(defmodule вҖҰ)` remain as permanent aliases. |
| `(assert cond "message")` | 2 | Record a pass/fail entry. Failures surface in the review report, never aborts the build. |
| `(assert-range value lo hi "label")` | 4 | Record an assertion that `value` is in `[lo, hi]`, with a formatted diagnostic. |
| `(fmt "template" argsвҖҰ)` | 1+ | Format a string. See the вҖңString formatting directivesвҖқ table for the `~X` specifiers. |
| `(id <hex8>)` | вҖ” | Stable 8-char identifier auto-inserted by the build. Evaluator short-circuits to `.nil`. |

## Builtin operators

Arithmetic, comparison, and logic operators. Arguments are evaluated left-to-right before the operator runs.

| Form | Summary |
| --- | --- |
| `(+ a b)` | Numeric addition. |
| `(- a b)` | Numeric subtraction. |
| `(* a b)` | Numeric multiplication. |
| `(/ a b)` | Numeric division. Errors on divide-by-zero. |
| `(% a b)` | Numeric modulo. Errors on divide-by-zero. |
| `(> a b)` | Numeric greater-than вҶ’ boolean. |
| `(>= a b)` | Numeric greater-or-equal вҶ’ boolean. |
| `(< a b)` | Numeric less-than вҶ’ boolean. |
| `(<= a b)` | Numeric less-or-equal вҶ’ boolean. |
| `(== a b)` | Equality, defined for number-number and string-string. |
| `(!= a b)` | Inequality, defined for number-number and string-string. |
| `(and a b)` | Boolean and (eager вҖ” both args evaluated). |
| `(or a b)` | Boolean or (eager вҖ” both args evaluated). |
| `(not a)` | Boolean negation. |
| `(e96 r)` | Snap a resistance/number to the nearest E96 (1%) standard value. |

## String formatting directives

Specifiers accepted in a `(fmt "template" argsвҖҰ)` template. Each
consumes the next argument of the listed type (or none).

| Directive | Argument | Summary |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `~V` | number | Voltage: plain number + `V` (`3.41` вҶ’ `3.41V`). |
| `~R` | number | Resistance: SI-scaled with `k`/`M` (`4700` вҶ’ `4.7k`). |
| `~C` | number | Capacitance: SI-scaled `F`/`mF`/`uF`/`nF`/`pF` (`1e-7` вҶ’ `100nF`). |
| `~A` | number | Current: SI-scaled `A`/`mA`/`uA` (`0.002` вҶ’ `2mA`). |
| `~S` | string | Verbatim string argument. |
| `~~` | вҖ” | Literal `~` (consumes no argument). |

## Numeric literals

Bare numbers accept an SI scale suffix, optionally followed by one
unit letter that carries no scale (`100nF` == `100n`); a unit
letter may also stand alone (`3.3V` == `3.3`). The suffix must end
the token вҖ” `100kHz` falls through to a plain atom. Dimension
tokens are separate: `mm` keeps the value in millimetres and `mil`
converts to mm (1 mil = 0.0254 mm).

| Suffix | Scale | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `k` | 10^3 |  |
| `M` | 10^6 |  |
| `G` | 10^9 |  |
| `m` | 10^-3 | Only with a unit letter (`100mV`) вҖ” bare `3m`, `mm`, `mil` keep their dimension meanings. |
| `u` | 10^-6 |  |
| `n` | 10^-9 |  |
| `p` | 10^-12 |  |

Unit letters (scale-free): `V`, `A`, `F`, `H`, `R`.

## Design-scope forms

Forms that may appear directly inside a `(design-block вҖҰ)`, a
`(section вҖҰ)`, or a nested sub-section. The scope column lists
where each is accepted: **D** = design-block top level,
**S** = section, **s** = sub-section.

| Form | Scope | Summary |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `(instance "REF" component pinвҖҰ)` | DSs | Place a component with inline pin-to-net bindings. |
| `(port "name" [net] dir [kind] [(rated lo hi)] [(side left\|right\|top\|bottom)])` | DSs | Declare a block boundary signal. A power/rf port's direction (or an explicit (side вҖҰ)) tells the PCB rough placer where the net enters/leaves the module вҖ” in вҶ’ left, out вҶ’ right. |
| `(bus-port "prefix" width dir вҖҰ)` | DSs | Declare a multi-bit boundary bus that expands to one port per lane. |
| `(note "id" "text" [(ref вҖҰ)])` | DSs | Attach a design-time note to the surrounding scope. |
| `(section "name" ["subtitle"] formвҖҰ)` | DSs | Functional subsystem card. Inside `(section вҖҰ)` nests one level into a sub-section. |
| `(decouple "NET" [(comp "val")] COUNT per-pin [REF\|auto] PINвҖҰ)` | DSs | Emit COUNT decoupling caps per listed host pin. Component and REF may come from (decouple-defaults вҖҰ); a trailing `auto` expands to the pins already declared on the net. |
| `(series вҖҰ)` | DSs | Insert a series element (resistor / ferrite / etc.) between two nets. |
| `(fanout "COMMON" (comp) "NET1" "NET2" вҖҰ [(id вҖҰ)])` | DSs | Place one component from a shared COMMON net to each listed net (star of series elements). |
| `(net "A" "B" вҖҰ)` | DSs | Tie one or more nets to a canonical name (net-merge). |
| `(bus-net "PREFIX" lo hi "SUB") \| (bus-net "PREFIX" lo hi (suffixes вҖҰ) (over вҖҰ) (ports вҖҰ))` | DSs | Tie a lane range to a sub-block bus (1:1), or strided-fan-out the range across several sub-blocks' ports. |
| `(pins "REF" (group "label") pin-formвҖҰ)` | В·Ss | Group a main-IC's pin assignments under a sub-section. |
| `(protocol atom)` | В·Ss | Tag a section with a protocol keyword (e.g. `usb`, `i2c`). |
| `(calc вҖҰ)` | В·Ss | Inline design math block, surfaced in the review report. |
| `(description "text")` | В·Ss | One-line section description used in the review report and overview SVG. |
| `(role input\|output)` | В·SВ· | Tag a section as a block input or output for the overview diagram. |
| `(diagram hidden)` | В·SВ· | Opt this section out of the block-diagram view (schematic card still renders). |
| `(hosts "sub1" "sub2" вҖҰ)` | В·SВ· | Fold the named sub-blocks into this section's block-diagram node (explicit attachment). |
| `(category <key>)` | В·SВ· | Set this section's diagram category (e.g. mcu, power, rf), overriding the name heuristic. |
| `(group "name" ("R1" "R2" вҖҰ))` | DВ·В· | Bundle ref-des components for the schematic renderer's visual grouping pass. Members are a LIST of ref-des strings. NOTE: a different, unrelated (group вҖҰ) form lives inside (diagram-layout вҖҰ) вҖ” there it takes variadic block keys (section names / sub-block handles), e.g. (group "Label" "Block A" "Block B" вҖҰ), to cluster diagram blocks; that one is parsed inline by the layout form, not this registry entry. |
| `(function "name" ["caption"] [(stack N)] (hosts "Section A" "Section B" вҖҰ))` | DВ·В· | Hand-authored functional super-block for the top-level system view: groups the named sections/sheets into one what-it-does block (caption = verb/spec line, stack N = Г—N identical channels). The editor map draws these as the outermost zoom level. |
| `(sub-block "name" (module-call argsвҖҰ))` | DВ·В· | Instantiate a parameterised module inside the design. Its parts flatten into the netlist under the sub-block path prefix and the PCB solver places them with the rest of the board. |
| `(verifies (req "REF" REQID) [rationale])` | DВ·В· | Mark a requirement as satisfied by a specific instance. |
| `(test-point вҖҰ)` | DВ·В· | Declare a measurement / bring-up access point. |
| `(decouple-defaults (ic "REF") (bypass (comp)))` | DВ·В· | Set per-design decouple defaults: a fallback IC ref and bypass cap so (decouple вҖҰ) can omit both. |
| `(kicad-pcb "absolute/path/to/board.kicad_pcb")` | DВ·В· | Declare the PCB file the file-based KiCad sync writes board updates to. |
| `(stub "name" [(role вҖҰ)] [(mpn вҖҰ)] [(category key)] [(size W H)] [(channels N)] [(ref "REF")] (signal "name" class "net")вҖҰ)` | DВ·В· | Declare a placeholder part вҖ” auto-placed, sized bounding box, signal-wired, optionally N stacked channels вҖ” for design-phase diagrams before a real component exists. |
| `(diagram-layout (anchor "name") (place "name" (right-of\|left-of\|above\|below "ref"))вҖҰ)` | DВ·В· | Position blocks relative to one another on the SCHEMATIC block diagram (Mermaid-style, free-floating) вҖ” nothing to do with PCB placement, which is the force / rough solver on /pcb-layout. |
| `(board (size W H) [(corner-radius R)] (left\|right\|top\|bottom "REF"вҖҰ \| (rot N "REF")вҖҰ)вҖҰ [(corners "REF"вҖҰ)])` | DВ·В· | Physical board outline + edge hardware: (size W H) is the outline in mm (required вҖ” without it the form is inert). (corner-radius R) rounds the outline's corners with radius R mm вҖ” the shape flows to Edge.Cuts, the board-edge DRC, and every renderer as a fine polyline. Each (left\|right\|top\|bottom вҖҰ) list docks those parts flush INSIDE that board edge (the words name physical edges, not sides of an anchor), slid along the edge toward the pads they connect to; (rot N "REF") overrides the default pads-inward rotation. (corners вҖҰ) pins mounting hardware at the four corners (TL, TR, BR, BL in authored order). The force-solved interior placement is centered in the outline; the rendered views draw the outline rectangle. |
| `(revision "ID" [(date "YYYY-MM-DD")] [(change "ID" "summary")вҖҰ])` | DВ·В· | Declare the design's canonical board revision: a human-meaningful spin id ("A", "F4", "1.2"), an optional date, and an optional newest-first in-file changelog. Shown on the schematic header and review doc so a recipient of the .sexp can tell which revision they hold; bump it by hand when cutting a new spin. Distinct from the per-edit snapshot history вҖ” tag the git commit to anchor the bump. |
| `(rough [(anchor "REF")] (group "name" "REF"вҖҰ)вҖҰ)` | DВ·В· | Author the rough-placement seed (the `?rough=1` / "Rough" button on /pcb-layout): (anchor вҖҰ) names the IC everything centres on (default: the most-connected hub), and each (group вҖҰ) is a priority TIER in descending order вҖ” the first group is placed first and packs tightest to the anchor, later groups fan outward. A group sets priority, not position: every member still lands on the IC side its pad connects to (GND ignored), so a bypass cap sits by its VDD pad and a pull resistor by its signal pad. Parts in no group are placed last. Refs match by ref-des or module-local origin name (exact or leaf). |
| `(stackup N [(plane IDX "NET")вҖҰ] [(pour top\|bottom "NET")вҖҰ] [(thickness MM)])` | DВ·В· | Declare the board's copper stack: N total copper layers (1-based, 1 = top/F.Cu, N = bottom/B.Cu); each (plane IDX "NET") makes layer IDX a solid plane carrying NET, all other layers are routed signal layers. `(pour top\|bottom "NET")` is sugar for a plane on the matching OUTER layer (top = 1, bottom = N): the face is emitted as a solid copper pour (Gerber + /pcb-layout + PNG), NET pads already on that face connect through the pour with no stitching via, and signal routing prefers the un-poured face. An optional (thickness MM) sets the finished board thickness reported in the Gerber .gbrjob (default 1.6 mm). `(stackup 2)` is a plain 2-layer board with no planes вҖ” ground/power are routed as copper like any other net; `(stackup 2 (pour bottom "GND"))` is the classic 2-layer board with a bottom ground pour. Without the form the router keeps its legacy implicit model (4 layers with assumed GND+PWR inner planes). |
| `(net-class "name" [(width MM)] [(clearance MM)] [(via DIA DRILL)] [(priority 0-7)] (nets "A" "B"вҖҰ))` | DВ·В· | Routing geometry + routing order for the named nets: trace width, copper clearance, and via size (diameter + drill) in mm, plus a routing-priority tier вҖ” the autorouter routes higher tiers first, so a critical net (crystal, flash bus, a switcher's hot loop) claims its short path before a bulk rail can wall it off (the maze router has no rip-up; first-routed wins). Omitted numbers keep the router defaults (priority 0 = baseline); net names match the flattened netlist case-insensitively. The first class naming a net wins. Repeat the form for more classes (e.g. a wide "power" class and a tight "rf" class). |
| `(design-rules [(clearance MM)] [(min-drill MM)] [(mask-margin MM)] [(copper-edge MM)] [(hole-to-hole MM)] [(min-annular MM)] [(mask-web MM)] [(min-width MM)] [(track-width MM)] [(via DIA DRILL)])` | DВ·В· | Board-level DEFAULT design rules (all sub-forms optional; mm): (clearance) copper-to-copper spacing for the router + DRC; (min-drill) smallest legal drilled hole; (mask-margin) solder-mask opening expansion per pad side; (copper-edge) copper-to-board-outline clearance; (hole-to-hole) wall-to-wall spacing between two drilled holes; (min-annular) minimum via annular ring (copper radius вҲ’ drill radius); (mask-web) smallest solder-mask sliver between two adjacent openings; (min-width) narrowest legal track; (track-width) the default routed trace width; (via DIA DRILL) the default via copper diameter + drill. The last two seed the autorouter's geometry вҖ” an explicit query/panel override still wins for interactive routing, but the fab gate judges the board against these authored rules. All are global defaults вҖ” a per-net (net-class вҖҰ) still overrides width/clearance/via for its own nets. An omitted rule keeps the toolchain's built-in default (clearance 0.127, min-drill 0.2, mask-margin 0.05, copper-edge = clearance, hole-to-hole 0.25, min-annular 0.1, mask-web 0.2, min-width 0.1, track-width 0.127, via 0.4 / 0.2), so a design with no form is unchanged. |

## Section-name classifier keywords

The system-overview SVG picks each section's column + colour by
case-insensitive keyword match on the section **name**, walking
these rules in priority order (first hit wins). A section matching
no rule falls back to **connector** when it contains a J/P-prefixed
instance, else **peripheral**. An explicit `(category <key>)`
declaration overrides the heuristic.

| Category | Name keywords |
| --- | --- |
| mcu | `MCU`, `SoC`, `CPU`, `Core System`, `STM32`, `ESP32`, `nRF`, `Microcontroller` |
| power | `Buck`, `LDO`, `Regulator`, `Power`, `Charger`, `Converter`, `PMIC` |
| memory | `Flash`, `PSRAM`, `RAM`, `EEPROM`, `SD Card` |
| clock | `Clock`, `HSE`, `LSE`, `Oscillator`, `PLL`, `Crystal` |
| comms | `USB`, `Ethernet`, `BLE`, `WiFi`, `CAN`, `UART` |
| sensor | `IMU`, `ADC`, `Sensor`, `Temperature`, `Accelerometer`, `Gyro` |
| analog | `Analog`, `DAC`, `Op-Amp`, `Reference`, `Amplifier` |
| protection | `ESD`, `Protection`, `Fuse`, `TVS` |
| connector | `Connector`, `Expansion`, `Header`, `Mounting`, `SWD`, `Debug`, `RJ45`, `B2B` |

Valid `(category <key>)` keys: `mcu`, `power`, `memory`, `peripheral`, `connector`, `clock`, `comms`, `sensor`, `analog`, `protection`.

## Requirement checks

Machine-checkable rules that hang off a library
`(requirement "text" (check вҖҰ))` form вҖ” the executable half of a
datasheet rule. Each is evaluated per placement of the part against
its **containing block** (a sub-block's check sees the sub-block's
nets, not the parent's). Pin references use the pinout **function
name** (`"VDD"`, `"EP"`) or a physical pad id. A rule is inherited by
every design instantiating the part; outcomes surface in `netlisp
check`, the review doc, and the MCP `run_checks` /
`list_component_requirements` tools. Author them with the
`add_component_requirement` MCP tool, whose `check` argument is one
`(check вҖҰ)` form using one of the primitives below.

| Check form | Asserts (against the containing block) |
| --- | --- |
| `(connected (pin "A") (pin "B"))` | Both named pins of this instance must resolve to the same net. |
| `(decoupling (pin "A") (pin "B") (min-uf F))` | At least one capacitor of value вүҘ F ВөF must bridge the nets on pins A and B. |
| `(pullup-range (pin "P") (net "N") (min-ohms L) (max-ohms H))` | A resistor of value in [L, H] О© must bridge pin P's net and net N. |
| `(voltage-range (pin "V") (min L) (max H))` | The voltage declared on pin V's net (via ports, walking DC-equivalent series parts) must lie in [L, H] V; a rated range must be a subset of it. |
| `(tied-to-net (pin "P") (net "N"))` | Pin P must resolve to net N (alias-aware) вҖ” a datasheet fixed-rail tie. |
| `(not-connected (pin "P"))` | Pin P must be left unconnected (no foreign co-pin, not a block port). |
| `(pin-not-floating (pin "P"))` | Pin P must be tied to a defined level: a net with a co-pin or a block port. |
| `(pins-on-same-net (pins "A" "B" вҖҰ))` | Every listed pin function must resolve to the same net (N-pin connected). |
| `(decoupling-per-pin (return-pin "GND") (pins "VDD_1" вҖҰ) (min-uf F) (count N))` | At least N of the listed pins must each have a вүҘ F ВөF cap to the return net. |
| `(series-element (kind R\|L\|C) (pin "P") (target-net "N") (min X) (max Y))` | An R/L/C of value in [X, Y] (О©/ВөH/ВөF by kind) must bridge pin P's net and N. |
